questions.question: Find the joint probability distribution P(X, Y) for the following events: X = {A, B, C} and Y = {0, 1}, given that P(X) = {P(A) = 1/3, P(B) = 1/3, P(C) = 1/3} and P(Y) = {P(0) = 1/2, P(1) = 1/2}.
questions.hint: Use the formula P(X, Y) = P(X) * P(Y | X)

questions.question: A fair coin is tossed twice. Find the joint probability distribution P(X, Y) for the outcomes of the coin tosses, where X is the result of the first toss and Y is the result of the second toss. Assuming the coin tosses are independent, what is the value of P(X, Y)?
questions.hint: Use the formula P(X, Y) = P(X) * P(Y | X)

questions.question: Two dice are rolled. Find the joint probability distribution P(X, Y) for the outcomes of the dice rolls, where X is the number on the first die and Y is the number on the second die. Calculate the probability of the event {X = 3, Y = 6} using the joint probability distribution.
questions.hint: List all possible outcomes and calculate the joint probabilities for each outcome
